name: Pulp Python (pulp_python)
vendor: Pulp Project (community, originated/sponsored by Red Hat)
cost: Free / open source (GPLv2+); no paid tier for the plugin itself. Pulp is embedded commercially in Red Hat Satellite and Ansible Automation Hub, but pulp_python is free to self-host.
language: Python (Django app; plugin on top of pulpcore, async aiohttp content app)
perf_notes: No official published benchmarks. The pulpcore "content app" that serves packages is async (aiohttp/gunicorn) and is designed to be run as multiple stateless replicas behind a load balancer; the API app and the distributed tasking workers scale independently. On-demand/lazy sync and pull-through caching avoid downloading the full PyPI dataset, reducing disk and bandwidth. Practical throughput is bounded by the chosen storage backend (filesystem/S3/Azure/GCS), PostgreSQL, and Redis rather than the plugin.
scale_notes: Horizontally scalable: API app, content app, and tasking workers are separate processes that scale out independently; shared state lives in PostgreSQL (required) plus Redis. HA achieved by running redundant replicas of each service plus an HA database. Deployable via the official Pulp Operator (Kubernetes/OpenShift) and community Helm charts. "Domains" provide multi-tenancy on a single instance. Used at large scale (e.g. Red Hat Satellite, Ansible Automation Hub / galaxy ecosystem). A full PyPI mirror is large; on-demand/pull-through modes are recommended at scale.
summary: A pulpcore plugin that turns a Pulp deployment into a full-featured private/mirrored PyPI index — hosting uploads, syncing/mirroring from upstream, on-demand pull-through caching, versioned repositories, RBAC, and modern Simple-API standards (PEP 503/629/658/691/700/714/740). PEP 592 yank is the one notable gap.
extras: Versioned repositories: every content change creates an immutable, restorable repository version (snapshot/rollback)
Content deduplication across repositories (content-addressed artifacts shared by sha256)
Pull-through caching can auto-save proxied content into a repository, and respects the remote's include/exclude filters
Package curation via includes/excludes filters on remotes (whitelist/blacklist which projects/versions sync)
OSV-backed vulnerability reports: scan a RepositoryVersion, get CVE/advisory data attached to the version and to individual package content units (pulp vulnerability-report create/show)
Automatic publish + distribute (autopublish) so new uploads/sync are immediately served; standalone publications were deprecated in 3.27.0 in favor of distribution-managed serving
Serve a specific (pinned) repository version of an index
Multi-tenancy via pulpcore Domains; model/domain/object-level RBAC roles
Rich tooling: REST API, pulp-cli, Ansible collection, and generated Python/Ruby client bindings
Genuinely multi-ecosystem: the same pulpcore instance can also host RPM, Debian, container, Ansible, OSTree, Maven, etc. via official sibling plugins
Remote storage import/export and content repair/checksum verification

PEP 740 provenance (added 3.22.0): upload attestation files; served as data-provenance in the PEP 503 index and a provenance key in the JSON Simple API, including Trusted Publisher identity. Note: this is provenance ingestion/serving only — Pulp does not itself implement OIDC Trusted Publishing token minting.
Modern Simple-API standards landed in recent releases: PEP 691 JSON simple (3.20.0), PEP 700 versions/size/upload-time (3.21.0), PEP 658/714 metadata exposure (3.23.0/3.24.0)

PEP 592 yank is NOT implemented: the simple-index renderer carries a 'TODO ... not implemented yet because it is mutable' note for data-yanked, and PythonPackageContent explicitly omits yanked/yanked_reason fields because they are mutable. Delete/removal of content is fully supported, but yanking is not.

Object storage via django-storages: filesystem, S3 (and S3-compatible: MinIO, Ceph/RADOS, Backblaze B2), and Azure Blob are documented in pulpcore; GCS is officially supported via the Pulp Operator (GKE + Workload Identity).
